rationale: Pre-conversion corporate name; matches 13-C MRSA usage of entity.name across other corporate forms (including MBCA-21 which uses the same key for the pre-conversion corporation).

label_verbatim: FIRST: ...the conversion of the corporation to a foreign unincorporated entity and the effective date of the conversion is (date)
rationale: Inline date blank for the effective date of the underlying entity conversion that triggered this charter surrender. Distinct from filing.date_signed (the surrender filing date) and from conversion.future_effective_date on MBCA-21 (which is an optional delayed-effectiveness for the conversion articles themselves).

label_verbatim: THIRD: The jurisdiction under the laws of which the surviving entity is organized is
rationale: State or country where the surviving foreign unincorporated entity is organized. Free-text; mirrors the shape of merger.surviving_entity.name_and_jurisdiction recital style but as a single jurisdiction-only field.

label_verbatim: FOURTH: If the surviving entity is a nonfiling entity, the address of its executive office immediately after the conversion.
rationale: Address of the surviving entity's executive office. Required only when the surviving entity is a nonfiling entity (e.g., a general partnership) — for filing entities (LLCs, LPs) the office address is captured by the surviving entity's own organic-document filing in its home jurisdiction. There is no checkbox to indicate nonfiling status; the rubric should treat this as conditionally required based on conversion.surviving_entity_jurisdiction + surviving entity type, otherwise optional.

label_verbatim: FIFTH: ...provide a mailing address to which the Secretary of State may mail a copy of any process served on the Secretary of State. (mailing address)
rationale: Mailing address for SOS-as-agent service of process specifically for shareholder appraisal-rights actions under 13-C chapter 13. Analogous to merger.foreign_survivor.service_of_process_mailing_address on MBCA-10 (which serves the same §1106 SOS-as-agent function for foreign mergers). Always required by FIFTH paragraph because the surviving entity is by definition foreign.

label_verbatim: *By ___ (signature of an officer or other duly authorized representative) / (type or print name and capacity)
rationale: Combined name+capacity widget. Shape D (matches MBCA-11, MLLC-12, MBCA-21, MBCA-21A). Form footnote authorizes 'an officer or other duly authorized representative' per 13-C MRSA §956.1.

label_verbatim: Name of entity(s) on the submitted filings [1]
rationale: Cover-letter row 1 — typically populated with the surrendering corporation's name.

widget_id: Name of entitys on the submitted filings 2
rationale: Cover-letter row 2 — optional second entity (e.g., the surviving foreign entity for SOS routing if multiple filings are bundled).

label_verbatim: Hold attested copy for pick up

rationale: Total fees ($90 base per page-0 header, plus any expedite premium).

widget_id: Contact email address for this filing
label_verbatim: Contact email address for this filing

widget_id: Email address to use for annual report reminders
label_verbatim: Email address to use for annual report reminders
rationale: Annual-report reminder email. Note: the surrendering corporation will not file annual reports post-surrender, but the cover letter still captures this field — likely a generic SOS cover-letter primitive that's harmless when populated.
